description: 'luera' gathers time before the present for memory, record, and discussion. As a noun, it makes earlier time itself the subject; the particle 'to' separately places a clause there.
articulatory_notes: Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for 'l' and draw it back into high-back rounded 'u'. Unround and bring the tongue forward for the separate stressed middle-height 'e'. Tap or briefly trill the tongue, then open the jaw into central 'a'.

semantic_domains.temporal: earlier time made available as a subject of memory, record, or thought
